arts.ttl: Report: What Trent Alexander-Arnold wants Real Madrid to do now after their opening contract offer falls short
Is Rodrygo the most underrated player in the Champions League?
arts.sum: With three LaLiga titles, two Champions Leagues and a goal every two-and-a-half games, Rodrygo deserves far more plaudits than he gets.

arts.ttl: Jude Bellingham equals Lionel Messi Champions League record after starring in Real Madrid's 5-1 win over Red Bull Salzburg
arts.sum: Jude Bellingham has equalled one of Lionel Messi 's legendary Champions League records after playing a starring role in Real Madrid 's 5-1 win over RB Salzburg.

arts.ttl: Three observations from Real Madrid’s 5-1 win vs RB Salzburg
arts.sum: It made me wonder — how close is this starting XI to Real Madrid’s best XI when it comes to keeping the ball, injured players aside? There have been doubts all season about whether Real Madrid’s multi-talented quartet of Rodrygo, Jude Bellingham, Vinicius Jr and Kylian Mbappe would work or not.

arts.ttl: Vinicius Junior Reacts to Scoring His 100th Goal for Real Madrid
arts.sum: Vinícius Júnior scored his 100th goal for Real Madrid, becoming the 23rd player in club history to accomplish the feat.

arts.ttl: Rodrygo and Vinícius double up as Real Madrid hit five past hapless Salzburg
arts.sum: “We will play and then we will see,” Carlo Ancelotti had said, so Real Madrid did their part and by the end of an ultimately easy night, life looked good again. The European champions started this match as the 22nd best team in the continent, somewhere towards the bottom of a table that keeps everyone guessing and supposedly on the edge of a catastrophe, and finished it six places higher and feeling as if they are among the favourites again. If, the coach was keen to stress, they can defend better. Madrid had been defeated by Lille, Milan and Liverpool, but there was time still and a revival to come and now a 5-1 victory over Red Bull Salzburg in their penultimate game has lifted them back among the seeds for the playoff round. With one game to go, against Brest next week, and the team taking shape, it also leaves them a single point off the top eight. Two goals each from Rodrygo and Vinícius and another for Kylian Mbappé mean Ancelotti’s side still have a small chance of completing the first phase of this new format going straight through to the last 16, although seven teams still stand between them and that target. arts.ttl: Vinícius Júnior reaches 100 goals with Real Madrid as club advances in Champions League
arts.sum: Vinícius scored his 99th and 100th goals, according to UEFA, moving him into third place in the top Brazilian scorers in the Champions League with 28 goals in the competition. Rodrygo has scored five goals in his last four matches, while Vinícius has three in his last two games.
